activities.d: Dubai dazzles with the world's tallest building, the Burj Khalifa, plus luxury shopping and futuristic architecture.
A desert safari of dune-bashing, camel rides and a Bedouin-style dinner is the classic Dubai add-on.
Dubai pairs warm-water beaches with mega-malls — one even has an indoor ski slope.
Dubai's nightlife runs to glitzy rooftop lounges and beach clubs (alcohol served in licensed venues).

hazards.note: Extreme heat — daytime highs of 105-115F / 40-46C

seasonal.d: Nov-March is the comfortable season for beaches and outdoor sightseeing, before the extreme summer heat.

AE.summary: Skyscrapers, shopping, desert safaris and beaches.

AF.summary: Hindu Kush scenery and ancient Silk Road heritage — under a Do Not Travel advisory; effectively closed to tourism.

AL.summary: Riviera beaches and mountain villages at Balkan prices.

AM.summary: Ancient monasteries, mountain scenery and brandy in one of the world's oldest Christian nations.

AO.summary: Vast empty beaches, desert meeting ocean, and rebounding wildlife.

activities.d: Buenos Aires is all about late-night steakhouses, Malbec and sultry tango in barrios like San Telmo.
El Chalten is Argentina's trekking capital, beneath the jagged Fitz Roy peaks in Patagonia.
Mendoza is the heart of Argentine wine — Malbec tastings with the snow-capped Andes as a backdrop.
The Argentine side of Iguazu Falls puts you right above the Devil's Throat on walkways over the cascades.

seasonal.d: Patagonia's trekking season is the southern summer, roughly November-March, when the weather is mildest.
Jun-Dec, southern right whales gather to breed off Peninsula Valdes on the Atlantic coast.

AR.summary: Buenos Aires, Patagonia trekking, wine country and glaciers.

activities.d: Vienna's Habsburg palaces — Schonbrunn and the Hofburg — recall its imperial grandeur.
Salzburg and the lake-and-mountain village of Hallstatt are Austria's most scenic spots.
The Tyrol is a premier ski region, with charming resort towns like Innsbruck and Kitzbuhel.
Vienna's grand coffee houses and classical-music heritage (Mozart, the New Year's Concert) define its culture.

seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Sep) is for Alpine hiking and swimming in the clear Salzkammergut lakes.
December-April is ski season in the Tyrolean and Arlberg Alps.
Vienna and Salzburg host some of Europe's most atmospheric Advent Christmas markets in December.

AT.summary: Vienna's music and cafes, alpine lakes and ski villages.

hazards.note: Cyclone and box-jellyfish season in the tropical north (Cairns, Darwin)

AU.summary: Reef and beaches, the Outback, and big coastal cities.

AZ.summary: Baku's old city and flame towers, mud volcanoes and Caucasus villages.

BA.summary: Ottoman bridges, riverside old towns and raw Dinaric Alps scenery at low prices.

hazards.note: Monsoon — widespread flooding is common

BD.summary: River-delta life, the world's longest natural beach and the Sundarbans mangroves.

activities.d: Bruges and Ghent are exquisitely preserved medieval cities of canals, gabled houses and belfries.
Belgium is a world capital of beer and chocolate — sample both around Brussels's grand market square.
Antwerp blends Rubens-era art with a cutting-edge fashion and diamond scene.
Brussels has a relaxed bar scene built around hundreds of distinctive Belgian beers.

seasonal.d: Late spring to early autumn is the most pleasant time for Belgium's walkable cities.
December brings festive Christmas markets and lights to Brussels and Bruges.

BE.summary: Medieval squares, beer, chocolate and easy train hops.

BF.summary: West African crafts, music and mud-brick architecture — currently under a Do Not Travel advisory.

BG.summary: Black Sea beaches, monasteries and cheap ski slopes.

BI.summary: Lake Tanganyika shores, drumming traditions and green highlands.

BJ.summary: Birthplace of vodun, stilt villages and the Slave Route's poignant history.

BN.summary: Gilded mosques, water villages and pristine Bornean rainforest in a tiny sultanate.

hazards.note: Altiplano rainy season — salt-flat and back-road travel gets difficult

BO.summary: The Uyuni salt flats, high-altitude cities and Lake Titicaca.

activities.d: Rio's Copacabana and Ipanema beaches sit beneath Christ the Redeemer and Sugarloaf Mountain.
Rio and Salvador throw the world's biggest Carnival and keep up samba-fuelled nightlife year-round.
Manaus is the gateway to Amazon riverboat trips and jungle lodges deep in the rainforest.
Iguazu Falls is a thundering 2.7km wall of cascades through the jungle on the Argentine border.

seasonal.d: Carnival explodes for the days before Lent (Feb or early March) — Brazil's wildest celebration.
December-March is Brazilian summer — hot, festive beach season around New Year and Carnival.
The dry season (roughly Jul-Oct) is prime for spotting jaguars and wildlife in the Pantanal wetlands.

BR.summary: Rio beaches, the Amazon, Iguazú Falls and Carnival.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane season

BS.summary: Swimming pigs, blue holes and 700 islands of postcard Caribbean.

hazards.note: Monsoon — views disappear and trails turn muddy

BT.summary: Cliffside monasteries and Himalayan trails in the kingdom of Gross National Happiness (daily visitor fee applies).

hazards.note: Rainy season — game viewing is at its hardest

BW.summary: The Okavango Delta — Africa's great water safari.

BY.summary: Soviet-era boulevards, castles and primeval forest — currently under a Do Not Travel advisory.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane season

BZ.summary: The Belize Barrier Reef, cays and jungle ruins.

CA.summary: Rockies, lakes, wildlife and northern lights.

CD.summary: Gorillas and Nyiragongo's lava lake in Africa's wildest park — largely off-limits under a Do Not Travel advisory.

CF.summary: Forest elephants and lowland gorillas at Dzanga-Sangha — under a Do Not Travel advisory.

CG.summary: Gorillas and untouched rainforest along the other Congo's riverbanks.

activities.d: The Swiss Alps offer world-class hiking beneath icons like the Matterhorn at Zermatt and the Jungfrau at Interlaken.
Switzerland's panoramic trains, like the Glacier Express, are sightseeing in themselves.
Lakeside Lucerne, with its covered wooden bridge and mountain backdrop, is the classic Swiss postcard.
St Moritz and Zermatt are blue-chip ski resorts, with glacier runs open even in summer.

seasonal.d: Roughly June-September is Alpine hiking season, once the snow clears from the high trails.
December-April is ski season across the Swiss Alps, with glacier areas open longer.

CH.summary: Alps, lakes, scenic trains, hiking and skiing.

hazards.note: Peak rainy season

CI.summary: Surf beaches, the world's biggest basilica and Abidjan's music scene.

CM.summary: "Africa in miniature": volcano hikes, beaches, rainforest and Sahel in one country.

hazards.note: Peak heat and humidity, with heavy summer rains in the south and east

CN.summary: The Great Wall, imperial cities, karst landscapes and megacity life.

activities.d: Cartagena pairs a colourful walled colonial old town with Caribbean beaches and nearby islands.
Medellin's spring-like climate fuels famous nightlife, and Cali is the salsa-dancing capital of the world.
The lush Coffee Triangle around Salento offers farm tours and the towering wax palms of the Cocora Valley.
Tayrona National Park's jungle-backed Caribbean beaches are among Colombia's most beautiful.

hazards.note: First rainy season
Second, heavier rainy season

seasonal.d: Dec-Mar (plus a drier spell around Jul-Aug) are the sunniest windows to travel most of the country.

CO.summary: Cartagena, coffee region, Caribbean coast and Andes.

activities.d: Arenal volcano anchors a region of natural hot springs, waterfalls and adventure activities.
The Pacific Guanacaste coast around Tamarindo has warm water, sunset surf and lively beach towns.
Monteverde's misty cloud forest, crossed by hanging bridges, teems with birds, sloths and monkeys.
Costa Rica pioneered the rainforest zip-line canopy tour, now a national adventure staple.

hazards.note: Wettest months of the year on the Pacific side

seasonal.d: Dec-April is the dry season — the sunniest, most reliable overall window across most of the country.
Jul-Oct, green sea turtles nest by the thousand on the Caribbean beaches of Tortuguero.

CR.summary: Rainforest, volcanoes, surfing and wildlife — the classic eco-adventure.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane season

CU.summary: Havana's old town and classic cars, tobacco valleys and cays.

CY.summary: Mediterranean beaches, mountain monasteries and 10,000 years of layered history.

activities.d: Prague's fairytale Old Town Square, Charles Bridge and hilltop castle are among Europe's best-preserved.
Prague has lively nightlife and the world's cheapest great beer in traditional Czech beer halls.
Karlovy Vary is an elegant 19th-century spa town of grand colonnades and thermal springs.
Cesky Krumlov is a perfectly preserved medieval town wrapped in a bend of the Vltava river.

seasonal.d: Spring and autumn bring mild weather and thinner crowds — ideal for wandering Prague's lanes.
December lights up Prague's Old Town Square with one of Central Europe's prettiest Christmas markets.

CZ.summary: Prague's old town and castles, beer culture and spa towns.

activities.d: Berlin wears its 20th-century history openly — the Wall, Brandenburg Gate and Museum Island.
Berlin is Europe's techno capital, home to legendary clubs like Berghain that run all weekend.
Bavaria's fairytale Neuschwanstein castle (the Disney inspiration) sits amid Alpine foothills.
Munich is beer-hall country, peaking at the world's biggest beer festival, Oktoberfest.

seasonal.d: Mid-September to early October, Munich's Oktoberfest draws millions for beer tents and lederhosen.
December fills German town squares with mulled-wine Christmas markets, among the best in Europe.

DE.summary: Cities, castles, beer halls and Christmas markets.

DJ.summary: Salt lakes below sea level, alien volcanic landscapes and whale sharks.

activities.d: Copenhagen is a bike-friendly capital of clean Scandinavian design, from Nyhavn's harbour to the Little Mermaid.
Copenhagen's nightlife centres on the Meatpacking District and cosy candle-lit bars.
Helsingor's Kronborg Castle is the real-life setting of Shakespeare's Hamlet, a short trip from the city.
Foodie Copenhagen (home of Noma) has buzzing food halls and the historic Tivoli Gardens amusement park.

seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Aug) brings the warmest weather and long daylight — peak time for Copenhagen's outdoor life.

DK.summary: Copenhagen's design, food scene and cycling life.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane season

DO.summary: All-inclusive beaches plus waterfalls and whales beyond the resorts.

DZ.summary: Roman ruins without crowds, Casbah alleys and the grandest Sahara scenery.

hazards.note: Coastal rainy season (the highlands stay milder)

EC.summary: Galapagos wildlife, Andean markets and Amazon lodges in one compact country.

EE.summary: A fairy-tale medieval old town, bog walks and Europe's most digital society.

activities.d: The Pyramids of Giza and the Sphinx — the last surviving ancient wonder — sit right on Cairo's edge.
A Nile cruise between Luxor and Aswan links Karnak temple and the tombs of the Valley of the Kings.
The Red Sea off Hurghada and Sharm el-Sheikh offers warm, coral-rich diving year-round.
Cairo's new Grand Egyptian Museum showcases Tutankhamun's treasures beside the pyramids.

hazards.note: Intense desert heat — Luxor and Aswan regularly exceed 105F / 40C

seasonal.d: Oct-Apr is the comfortable season for Cairo and Luxor sightseeing, dodging the brutal summer heat.
The Red Sea is divable all year, with the warmest water and calmest seas in the warmer months.

EG.summary: Pyramids, Nile cruises and Red Sea diving.

EH.summary: Atlantic-meets-Sahara emptiness; kitesurfing at Dakhla lagoon (disputed territory — check status).

ER.summary: Asmara's perfectly preserved Art Deco streets and a Red Sea time capsule.

activities.d: Barcelona is Gaudi's playground — the still-unfinished Sagrada Familia basilica and the mosaic-covered Park Guell are the must-sees.
Madrid parties till sunrise, while Ibiza is the global capital of superclubs and beach raves each summer.
The southern Costa del Sol delivers reliable sun and resort beaches, with Andalusian cities like Malaga close by.
Seville is the soul of southern Spain — tapas bar-hopping and passionate flamenco in the Triana quarter.

hazards.note: Heatwaves — Seville and the interior regularly top 104F / 40C

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is beach season on the Balearic islands and mainland coast — hot, dry and busiest in August.
Late August brings Bunol's La Tomatina tomato fight, the headline of Spain's packed summer festival calendar.

ES.summary: Beaches, tapas, Gaudí and flamenco; islands in summer.

hazards.note: Kiremt rains — the highlands are wet and road travel is slow

ET.summary: Rock-hewn churches, highland treks and coffee culture.

FI.summary: Saunas, lake country and Lapland winters.

hazards.note: South Pacific cyclone season

FK.summary: Penguin colonies outnumbering people on windswept South Atlantic moors.

activities.d: Paris headlines with the Eiffel Tower, the Louvre's Mona Lisa and Notre-Dame, all linked by walkable riverside neighbourhoods.
Paris nightlife runs from wine bars in Le Marais to late cabarets and clubs around Pigalle and Oberkampf.
Bordeaux and Burgundy are France's great wine regions — chateau tastings and vineyard cycling, best around the autumn harvest.
The French Riviera around Nice, Cannes and Monaco offers Mediterranean beaches, glamour and a famously sunny microclimate.

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is Riviera beach season — warm, dry and lively, peaking in busy, pricey August.
Dec-Apr is ski season in the French Alps, home to huge linked resorts like the Three Valleys.
Provence's lavender fields bloom purple from late June into July, best around Valensole and the Luberon.

FR.summary: Paris, wine country, the Riviera and the Alps.

GA.summary: Surfing hippos' coast: forest elephants and whales where jungle meets the Atlantic.

activities.d: London's big hitters — the British Museum, Tower of London and Westminster — are mostly free and walkable along the Thames.
London nightlife spans historic pubs, Soho bars and world-class West End theatre and clubs.
The Scottish Highlands offer dramatic glens, lochs and castles, with Edinburgh's old town as the gateway.
Oxford and Cambridge are storybook university towns of honey-stone colleges, easy day trips from London.

seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Aug) brings the warmest, longest days — best for the countryside, though a shower is always possible.

GB.summary: London, castles, countryside and coastal walks.

GE.summary: Caucasus mountains, ancient wine country and Tbilisi's old town.

hazards.note: Harmattan — Saharan dust haze
Peak rainy season in the south

GH.summary: Coastal forts and history, beaches and Accra's music scene.

GL.summary: Icebergs the size of towns, dog sleds and midnight sun at the edge of the world.

GM.summary: Smiling-coast beaches and superb river birdwatching in Africa's smallest mainland nation.

GN.summary: Fouta Djallon's waterfalls and highlands — West Africa's water tower.

GQ.summary: Volcanic Bioko island rainforest, turtle beaches and Spanish-colonial Malabo.

activities.d: Santorini's caldera sunsets and Mykonos's beaches are the postcard Greece, linked by frequent island ferries.
Athens is anchored by the Acropolis and Parthenon, with the lively old Plaka quarter right at its foot.
Mykonos is the Aegean's party island — beach clubs by day, world-famous DJs by night.
The Cyclades and Ionian islands are a top sailing ground, hopping secluded coves by yacht or catamaran.

hazards.note: Heatwaves, wildfire risk, and peak island crowds

seasonal.d: Late May-September is island and sailing season — reliable sun and warm seas, busiest in August.

GR.summary: Ancient sites on the mainland, white-washed islands and sailing in summer.

hazards.note: Rainy season — daily afternoon downpours

GT.summary: Maya ruins in the jungle, volcano hikes and a stunning crater lake.

GW.summary: The wild Bijagós archipelago: saltwater hippos, turtles and timeless island culture.

GY.summary: Kaieteur's single-drop falls and giant otters in untouched rainforest.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane and heavy-rain season

HN.summary: Cheap Caribbean diving on Roatán and the Maya ruins of Copán.

activities.d: Walled Dubrovnik (Game of Thrones' King's Landing) and the islands of Hvar and Korcula define the Dalmatian coast.
Plitvice Lakes National Park is a chain of turquoise lakes and waterfalls linked by wooden walkways.
Split, built around the Roman Diocletian's Palace, is the launch point for sailing the Adriatic islands.
Hvar town is Croatia's summer party hub, with beach clubs and a yacht-set crowd.

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is coast and sailing season — warm Adriatic seas and sunshine, with peak crowds in August.

HR.summary: Adriatic coast, islands and walled old towns.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane season

HT.summary: The Citadelle fortress, vivid art and resilient culture — currently under a Do Not Travel advisory.

HU.summary: Budapest's thermal baths, ruin bars and Danube views.

activities.d: Bali pairs surf beaches and rice-terrace temples with the yoga-and-wellness scene around Ubud.
Near Yogyakarta, Borobudur is the world's largest Buddhist temple, magical at sunrise.
Komodo and Raja Ampat rank among the planet's richest dive sites — and Komodo has its famous dragons.
Seminyak and Canggu are Bali's nightlife strip, full of beach clubs and sunset bars.

hazards.note: Wet season on Java and Bali — daily downpours

seasonal.d: Apr-Oct is the dry season — best for diving, surfing and volcano hikes across the islands.
The dry months bring prime manta-ray encounters and the calmest seas around Komodo.

ID.summary: Bali culture and surf, Komodo dragons, volcanoes and world-class diving.

activities.d: Dublin's pub culture — live trad music and pints of Guinness around Temple Bar — is the heart of a night out.
The 200m-high Cliffs of Moher are Ireland's most dramatic stretch of Atlantic coastline.
Killarney's lakes, national park and nearby castles showcase Ireland's lush green countryside.
The Ring of Kerry is a classic scenic loop drive of coast, mountains and villages.

seasonal.what: Long days, best odds of sun
seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Aug) brings the longest days and the best (if never guaranteed) odds of sunshine.
March 17th turns Dublin into a giant parade and party for St Patrick's Day.

IE.summary: Cliff coastlines, green countryside and pub culture.

activities.d: Jerusalem's walled Old City holds sites sacred to Judaism, Christianity and Islam within a few minutes' walk.
Tel Aviv is a Mediterranean beach city with a famously round-the-clock nightlife and food scene.
Float effortlessly in the ultra-salty Dead Sea, the lowest point on Earth, then slather on its mineral mud.
The Negev desert holds the clifftop fortress of Masada, best climbed for sunrise.

hazards.note: Peak summer heat, especially around the Dead Sea and Eilat

seasonal.d: Spring and autumn are the mildest, most comfortable seasons for sightseeing.

IL.summary: Jerusalem's old city, Tel Aviv beaches and the Dead Sea.

activities.d: The Golden Triangle links the Taj Mahal in Agra with Delhi and the pink forts and palaces of Jaipur.
South India offers Kerala's tranquil houseboat backwaters and Goa's beaches and nightlife.
Varanasi on the Ganges is Hinduism's holiest city — sunrise boat rides past the riverside ghats are unforgettable.
The Himalayan foothills around Rishikesh are India's yoga and trekking hub on the upper Ganges.

hazards.note: Pre-monsoon heat — the plains regularly top 110F / 43C
Southwest monsoon — heavy rain across most of the country

seasonal.d: Nov-Feb is the cool, dry, comfortable window for North India and Rajasthan.
Spring (Mar-May) gives the best tiger sightings, as heat draws wildlife to the waterholes.
March's Holi is the joyous 'festival of colours,' celebrated nationwide with thrown powder and water.

IN.summary: Forts and palaces, the Taj Mahal, Himalayas, beaches in Goa and tiger safaris.

IQ.summary: Babylon, Ur and the marshes of Mesopotamia; Kurdistan's mountains are the most-visited corner.

IR.summary: Isfahan's turquoise domes, Persepolis and legendary hospitality — under a Do Not Travel advisory for US citizens.

activities.d: The Golden Circle loops geysers, the Gullfoss waterfall and a continental rift, all near Reykjavik.
From autumn to spring, Iceland's dark skies make it one of the best places on Earth to see the aurora.
The geothermal Blue Lagoon's milky-blue waters are a classic soak near the airport.
The Ring Road circles the whole island past waterfalls, glaciers, black-sand beaches and fjords.

hazards.note: Storm season — short daylight and frequent road closures

seasonal.d: September-April are the dark months when the aurora is visible on clear nights.
Summer brings near-24-hour daylight and opens the interior highland (F-road) routes.
November-March, you can tour the electric-blue natural ice caves beneath Vatnajokull glacier.

IS.summary: Waterfalls, volcanoes, glaciers, northern lights and the Ring Road.

activities.d: Rome is an open-air museum — the Colosseum, Roman Forum and Pantheon all sit within a walkable historic centre.
Florence holds Renaissance icons like Michelangelo's David, while Venice is a car-free maze of canals and palazzos.
The Amalfi Coast's cliffside villages — Positano, Ravello — and the isle of Capri are Italy's most scenic shoreline.
Tuscany pairs rolling vineyards and Chianti wine with hilltop towns like Siena and San Gimignano.

hazards.note: Heatwaves and the year's biggest crowds

seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Sep) is beach season on the coast and islands, though Jul-Aug brings heat and the biggest crowds.
Autumn (Oct-Nov) is white-truffle season in Piedmont, centred on the famous Alba truffle fair.

IT.summary: Art cities, ruins, coastline and food regions end to end.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane season

activities.d: Petra, the rose-red city carved into desert cliffs, is reached on foot through a narrow canyon to the Treasury.
Wadi Rum's red sand and towering rock formations (a frequent Mars film stand-in) are best as an overnight camp.
Jordan's Dead Sea shore lets you float in the mineral-rich water at Earth's lowest point.
Jerash is one of the best-preserved Roman provincial cities, with colonnaded streets and theatres.

hazards.note: Peak desert heat — tough for hiking Petra

seasonal.d: Spring and autumn bring mild temperatures ideal for Petra and the desert, avoiding the summer heat.

JO.summary: Petra, Wadi Rum desert and the Dead Sea.

activities.d: Kyoto alone has over 1,600 temples and shrines — walk Fushimi Inari's endless vermilion torii gates and the gold-leafed Kinkaku-ji pavilion.
Tokyo and Osaka serve world-class sushi, ramen and izakaya crawls, with neon nightlife in Shinjuku and Dotonbori going past midnight.
Soak in volcanic hot-spring baths at a traditional ryokan inn in Hakone, many with open-air views toward Mount Fuji.
Hokkaido and Nagano get some of the planet's lightest powder snow Dec-Mar, plus the famous hot-spring snow monkeys near Nagano.

hazards.note: Tsuyu rainy season across most of the country
Typhoon season — peak in September

seasonal.d: The sakura bloom sweeps north over a few weeks in late Mar-Apr — peak hanami picnics last only about a week in each city.
November turns Kyoto's maples and temple gardens fiery red and gold — the autumn answer to cherry-blossom season.
Deep, dry powder from December through March makes Hokkaido and Nagano a world-class winter-sports destination.

JP.summary: Temples, food, and bullet-train city-hopping; cherry blossoms in spring, snow sports in winter.

hazards.note: Long rains — safari tracks get muddy

KE.summary: Safari and the Great Migration; beaches on the coast.

KG.summary: Yurt stays, alpine lakes and horse treks across the Tian Shan.

hazards.note: Wet season — afternoon downpours; remote temple roads get muddy

KH.summary: Angkor Wat and Khmer temples, river life and southern islands.

KP.summary: The world's most closed state; tours (when permitted) are tightly controlled — US travel is prohibited.

activities.d: Seoul blends Joseon-era palaces like Gyeongbokgung with hyper-modern districts and street food.
Hongdae and Gangnam drive Seoul's nightlife — Korean BBQ, soju bars and clubs till dawn.
Coastal Busan has city beaches, bustling seafood markets and the colourful hillside village of Gamcheon.
Seoraksan National Park's granite peaks and temples are spectacular, especially in autumn.

hazards.note: Jangma rainy season — short but intense

seasonal.d: Early-to-mid April brings cherry blossoms nationwide, with festivals in Seoul, Busan and Jinhae.
October-November turns Korea's mountains brilliant red and gold — prime hiking season.
December-February is ski season near Pyeongchang, host of the 2018 Winter Olympics.

KR.summary: Seoul's food and nightlife, palaces, K-culture and mountain temples.

hazards.note: Among the hottest summers on Earth — 115F / 46C is routine

KW.summary: Gulf city of souqs and towers with strong food culture and desert camps.

KZ.summary: Steppe, canyons and alpine lakes the size of seas, plus futuristic Astana.

hazards.note: Smoke season — agricultural burning blankets the north in haze
Monsoon — heavy rain and rough rural roads

LA.summary: Slow Mekong towns, waterfalls and monasteries.

LB.summary: Roman Baalbek, Beirut's nightlife and superb food between mountains and sea.

hazards.note: Southwest monsoon hits the south and west beaches
Northeast monsoon hits the east coast — pick your coast by season

LK.summary: Beaches, tea country, safari and ancient cities.

LR.summary: Point-break surf at Robertsport and chimpanzee island sanctuaries.

LS.summary: The "Kingdom in the Sky": pony treks and Africa's only ski slopes.

LT.summary: Baroque Vilnius, the eerie Hill of Crosses and Baltic dunes.

LU.summary: A fortress capital over dramatic gorges, castles and Moselle wines.

LV.summary: Art Nouveau Riga, wide Baltic beaches and castle-dotted river valleys.

LY.summary: Leptis Magna's epic Roman ruins and Saharan ghost towns — under a Do Not Travel advisory.

activities.d: Marrakesh and Fez have labyrinthine walled medinas and souks — sensory overload in the best way.
From Merzouga you ride camels into the Erg Chebbi dunes and sleep in a Sahara desert camp.
Breezy, laid-back Essaouira is a walled Atlantic port town known for windsurfing and seafood.
The High Atlas offers Berber-village treks, including North Africa's highest peak, Mount Toubkal.

hazards.note: Interior heat — Marrakech and the desert top 100F / 38C

seasonal.d: Spring and autumn are ideal for the imperial cities and Atlas trekking, between winter cold and summer heat.
Oct-Apr brings tolerable daytime desert temperatures for Sahara excursions.

MA.summary: Medinas, the Sahara and the Atlas Mountains.

MD.summary: The world's biggest wine cellars and monasteries carved into cliffs.

ME.summary: A fjord-like bay, walled towns and black-mountain canyons in pocket size.

hazards.note: Cyclone season — the east coast takes direct hits most years

MK.summary: Lake Ohrid's ancient churches and bazaar towns between mountains.

ML.summary: Djenné's great mud mosque, Dogon country and Timbuktu's legend — under a Do Not Travel advisory.

hazards.note: Burning-season haze upcountry
Southwest monsoon — heaviest on the coast

MM.summary: Bagan's temple plains and Inle Lake (check the security situation first).

MN.summary: Steppe horse treks, ger camps and the Gobi.

MR.summary: The iron-ore train, ancient caravan libraries and dune seas.

hazards.note: Cyclone season

MU.summary: Lagoon beaches, reef snorkeling and a green volcanic interior.

MW.summary: The 'warm heart of Africa': a freshwater lake like a sea, with cichlid snorkeling.

activities.d: The Caribbean coast — Cancun, Tulum, Playa del Carmen — pairs white-sand beaches and cenote swimming holes with high-energy nightlife.
Chichen Itza's El Castillo pyramid is one of the New Seven Wonders, an easy day trip from the Yucatan beaches.
Mexico City delivers world-class food from tacos to fine dining, Frida Kahlo's house-museum, and the Teotihuacan pyramids nearby.
Oaxaca is the heartland of Mexican cuisine — mole, mezcal and a spectacular Day of the Dead.

hazards.note: Hurricane season peaks on both coasts

seasonal.d: Snorkel alongside gentle whale sharks off Isla Mujeres and La Paz, roughly June-September.
The dry season (Nov-Apr) brings calm, clear Caribbean water — prime reef snorkeling and diving.
Dec-Apr, gray whales calve in Baja's Pacific lagoons, where small boats get remarkably close.
From November to March, millions of migrating monarch butterflies blanket the fir forests of Michoacan.

MX.summary: Caribbean beaches, cenotes and Maya ruins on the Yucatán; food and art in the cities.

hazards.note: Northeast monsoon — east-coast islands (Perhentians, Tioman) largely shut down

MY.summary: Islands and diving, rainforest, and a great food scene.

hazards.note: Cyclone season on the coast

MZ.summary: Dhow sails, archipelago sandbanks and some of the Indian Ocean's best diving.

NA.summary: Sossusvlei dunes, desert-adapted wildlife and huge skies.

NC.summary: The world's largest lagoon, heart-shaped mangroves and Melanesian-French culture.

NE.summary: Saharan massifs, salt caravans and the Cure Salée festival — under a Do Not Travel advisory.

hazards.note: Harmattan dust haze
Rainy season

NG.summary: Lagos's unstoppable music and art scene, sacred groves and ancient cities.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane and heavy-rain season

NI.summary: Volcano boarding, colonial Granada and twin-volcano Ometepe island.

activities.d: Amsterdam's 17th-century canal ring and museums (Van Gogh, the Anne Frank House) are the heart of a visit.
Amsterdam's nightlife is famously liberal — brown cafes, canal-side bars and big clubs.
The flat, bike-first Netherlands is made for cycling — rent a bike to explore towns like Utrecht and Haarlem.
Traditional open-air cheese markets in Gouda and Alkmaar are a quirky, photogenic morning out.

seasonal.d: Late March to mid-May, the Keukenhof gardens and surrounding bulb fields explode with millions of tulips.
Summer brings the warmest weather and long daylight — peak season for canal terraces and festivals.

NL.summary: Canals, cycling, museums — and tulips in spring.

activities.d: Norway's deep, cliff-walled fjords around Geiranger and Bergen are among the world's great landscapes.
Tromso, well above the Arctic Circle, is a prime base for chasing the northern lights.
The Flam Railway descends a steep fjord valley past waterfalls — a famously scenic train ride.
The Lofoten Islands have dramatic peaks rising straight from the sea, with hiking and red fishing villages.

seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Aug) brings mild weather, the midnight sun and the best fjord cruising and hiking.
Late September to March are the dark months for aurora viewing in the Arctic north.

NO.summary: Fjords, northern lights and midnight sun.

hazards.note: Monsoon — trekking trails are wet and mountain views are clouded

NZ.summary: Fjords, glaciers, hiking and adventure sports.

hazards.note: Extreme heat in the lowlands (Salalah's khareef mist is the exception)

OM.summary: Wadis, dunes, forts and a dramatic fjord-like coast.

hazards.note: Wettest stretch of the rainy season

activities.d: The lost Inca city of Machu Picchu, reached by the multi-day Inca Trail or train from Cusco, is South America's top sight.
Lima is a global culinary capital, home to several of the world's 50 best restaurants and the birthplace of ceviche.
Iquitos, reachable only by boat or plane, is the gateway to Amazon jungle lodges and river cruises.
From Puno you visit the Uros people's floating reed islands on the world's highest navigable lake.

hazards.note: Highland rainy season — the Inca Trail closes every February

seasonal.d: May-September is the dry season — the best window for the Inca Trail and Andean hiking.
The classic Inca Trail closes each February for maintenance, though Machu Picchu itself stays open.

PE.summary: Machu Picchu and the Inca Trail, the Amazon and Lake Titicaca.

PG.summary: A thousand tribes, highland sing-sings, WWII wrecks and untouched reefs.

hazards.note: Typhoon season — peak storms Aug-Oct can disrupt island travel
Extreme pre-monsoon heat in the plains
Monsoon — flooding risk

PK.summary: Karakoram giants, the Hunza Valley and Mughal Lahore — checks advisories by region.

activities.d: Krakow's beautifully preserved medieval old town surrounds Europe's largest market square.
Poland holds sobering WWII history — the Auschwitz-Birkenau memorial and Warsaw's uprising museums.
Krakow's old Jewish Kazimierz quarter is packed with atmospheric, low-cost bars and clubs.
Zakopane, in the Tatra Mountains, is Poland's go-to hiking and skiing resort town.

seasonal.d: Late spring to early autumn offers the mildest weather for Poland's cities and mountains.
December brings charming Christmas markets to Krakow's and Warsaw's old squares.

PL.summary: Krakow and Gdansk old towns, mountain trails and hearty food.

PR.summary: Old San Juan's blue cobblestones, bioluminescent bays and rainforest — no passport needed for Americans.

PS.summary: Bethlehem, Jericho and Hebron's layered heritage — currently subject to severe travel warnings.

activities.d: Lisbon's hilly old town is best explored on the rattling Tram 28, past tiled facades and miradouro viewpoints.
The Algarve's golden cliffs and coves are Portugal's sunniest beach region, also great for learning to surf.
Porto is the home of port wine — tour the riverside cellars across the Douro from the old town.
Lisbon's Bairro Alto becomes one big open-air bar crawl at night, with riverside clubs in Cais do Sodre.

hazards.note: Heat and wildfire risk inland

seasonal.d: May-September is peak Algarve beach weather — warm, dry and sunny, busiest in August.
Autumn-winter (Oct-Mar) Atlantic swells make Nazare the home of record-breaking giant waves.

PT.summary: Lisbon and Porto, Algarve beaches, surfing and port wine.

PY.summary: Jesuit ruins, wetlands and an off-the-path feel.

hazards.note: Extreme heat — daytime highs of 105-115F / 40-46C

QA.summary: Doha's museums, souqs and desert day trips.

RO.summary: Transylvania castles, painted monasteries and Carpathian hikes.

RS.summary: Belgrade nightlife, fortress towns and hearty food.

RU.summary: Imperial St Petersburg, the Trans-Siberian and Kamchatka's volcanoes — under a Do Not Travel advisory.

hazards.note: Heaviest rainy season

RW.summary: Mountain gorillas, volcanoes and a famously clean capital.

hazards.note: Extreme desert heat

SA.summary: AlUla's tombs, Red Sea diving and fast-changing cities.

SB.summary: WWII wreck diving and village-stay island life far off the beaten path.

SD.summary: More pyramids than Egypt at Meroë, Nubian hospitality — currently in conflict; Do Not Travel.

activities.d: Stockholm spreads over 14 islands, pairing the medieval Gamla Stan old town with sleek Scandinavian design.
The Stockholm archipelago's thousands of islands are made for summer boat trips and swimming.
Swedish Lapland offers winter northern lights, dog-sledding and the original Icehotel.
Stockholm has a stylish nightlife scene, liveliest during the long, light summer nights.

seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Aug) brings long days and weather warm enough for the archipelago and outdoor life.
Sep-March, the dark northern skies of Lapland are best for catching the aurora.

SE.summary: Stockholm's archipelago, design and northern lights up north.

SG.summary: A food-obsessed city-state: hawker centres, gardens and shopping.

SI.summary: Lake Bled, Julian Alps and caves in a pocket-sized country.

SK.summary: High Tatras peaks, castles everywhere and storybook old towns at gentle prices.

SL.summary: Empty palm-lined beaches, chimp sanctuaries and a warm welcome rebuilding tourism.

hazards.note: Wet season

SN.summary: Dakar's music and markets, island history and surf.

SO.summary: A long Indian Ocean coast and frankincense history — under a Do Not Travel advisory (Somaliland differs in practice).

SR.summary: Dutch-colonial Paramaribo and pristine Amazonian interior lodges.

SS.summary: The world's youngest country; vast Sudd wetlands and huge migrations — Do Not Travel advisory.

SV.summary: World-class surf points, volcano hikes and the coffee-scented Ruta de las Flores.

SY.summary: Damascus, Aleppo and Palmyra — among history's great cities; Do Not Travel advisory.

SZ.summary: Walking safaris among rhinos and vibrant Swazi festivals in a tiny kingdom.

TD.summary: The Ennedi's rock arches and Zakouma's elephants — expedition territory; Do Not Travel advisory.

TF.summary: Subantarctic islands of penguins and seals — reachable only by research/supply ship from Réunion.

TG.summary: Voodoo markets, hilltop clay-castle villages and palm-lined Atlantic sand.

activities.d: The Andaman coast off Phuket and Krabi is dotted with limestone karst islands, clear water and some of Asia's best-value scuba diving.
Bangkok pairs gilded temples like Wat Pho's giant reclining Buddha with legendary street food on nearly every corner.
Bangkok's rooftop bars and Koh Phangan's beach full-moon parties anchor Thailand's famous nightlife.
Cooler, mountainous Chiang Mai is the base for jungle trekking, ethical elephant sanctuaries and hill-tribe villages.

hazards.note: Smoke season in the north — burning-season haze around Chiang Mai/Chiang Rai can hit hazardous air-quality levels
Tail of the rainy season — flooding possible, especially in the central plains

seasonal.d: Nov-Apr is the Andaman dry season — calm, clear water and ideal diving around Phuket and Krabi.
The Gulf islands (Koh Samui, Koh Phangan) stay driest roughly Jan-Sep, on a different rain cycle to the Andaman coast.
Mid-April's Songkran is the Thai new year — a nationwide three-day water fight in the streets.

TH.summary: Island beaches and diving in the south, temples and street food in Bangkok and the north.

TJ.summary: The Pamir Highway: high-altitude road-trip of a lifetime past turquoise lakes.

TL.summary: Whale migrations off Ataúro's reefs and coffee-country treks in Asia's newest nation.

TM.summary: The flaming Darvaza gas crater, marble Ashgabat and Silk Road Merv.

hazards.note: Peak summer heat away from the coast

TN.summary: Mediterranean beaches, Roman ruins and Saharan oases.

activities.d: Istanbul straddles two continents — the Hagia Sophia, Blue Mosque and labyrinthine Grand Bazaar are unmissable.
Cappadocia's surreal 'fairy chimney' valleys are best seen at dawn from a hot-air balloon.
Ephesus is one of the world's best-preserved classical cities, with its grand Roman library facade.
The turquoise Mediterranean coast around Antalya mixes resort beaches with ancient ruins.

hazards.note: Heatwaves and peak coastal crowds

seasonal.d: May-October is beach season on the Aegean and Mediterranean coasts — hot, dry and busiest mid-summer.
Spring and autumn give Cappadocia its calmest, clearest skies for ballooning.

TR.summary: Istanbul, Cappadocia balloons, ancient ruins and the turquoise coast.

TT.summary: Carnival's home, steelpan and doubles on Trinidad; reef-calm beaches on Tobago.

hazards.note: Typhoon season

TW.summary: Night markets, mountain gorges and hot springs around an easy rail loop.

hazards.note: Long rains — many safari camps close

UA.summary: Golden-domed Kyiv and Carpathian villages — currently at war; Do Not Travel advisory.

hazards.note: Heaviest rainy season

UG.summary: Gorillas, the Nile's source and big-game parks.

activities.d: The US has 63 national parks — the Grand Canyon, Yosemite and Yellowstone are bucket-list landscapes.
New York, with its skyline, museums and Broadway, is the iconic American city break.
Coastlines run from Florida and California to Hawaii, so there's somewhere warm almost year-round.
Vast distances make the US a road-trip country — historic Route 66 and the Pacific Coast Highway are classics.

seasonal.d: Late spring to early fall is when nearly all parks, including high-altitude roads, are fully open.
Late September into October paints New England and the Northeast in brilliant autumn colour.

US.summary: National parks, big cities and coast-to-coast variety.

UY.summary: Laid-back beaches, asado and colonial Colonia.

hazards.note: Desert heat — Samarkand and Bukhara regularly top 100F / 38C

UZ.summary: Silk Road cities: turquoise domes of Samarkand, Bukhara and Khiva.

VE.summary: Angel Falls and Caribbean cays — extraordinary nature under a Do Not Travel advisory.

activities.d: Ha Long Bay's thousands of limestone islets rising from emerald water are best seen on an overnight boat cruise.
Hanoi's chaotic Old Quarter is the place for pho, egg coffee and bia hoi on tiny plastic stools.
Ho Chi Minh City (Saigon) has Vietnam's liveliest nightlife — rooftop bars and the buzzing backpacker streets of Bui Vien.
Lantern-lit Hoi An is famous for its riverside old town and fast, affordable custom tailoring.

hazards.note: Typhoon and flooding season in central Vietnam (Hue, Hoi An, Da Nang)

seasonal.d: The central coast (Da Nang, Hoi An, Nha Trang) is driest and best for beaches roughly Feb-Aug.
The north (Hanoi, Sapa) is clearest and driest around Oct-Apr, with cool mountain trekking among rice terraces.

VN.summary: Halong Bay, street food, history and motorbike routes north to south.

hazards.note: South Pacific cyclone season

VU.summary: Stand at the rim of a live lava lake, then snorkel a sunken liner.

XK.summary: Europe's youngest country: Ottoman bazaars, monasteries and accursed-mountain hikes.

YE.summary: Sana'a's gingerbread towers and Socotra's alien dragon-blood trees — Do Not Travel; Socotra has separate charters.

ZA.summary: Safari, Cape Town, the Garden Route and whale watching.

hazards.note: Rainy season — many safari camps close

ZM.summary: Victoria Falls' Devil's Pool and the home of the walking safari.

ZW.summary: The thundering main face of Victoria Falls, Hwange's elephants and Great Zimbabwe.
